title: CandidateEvidence
description: Canonical evidence record for a single harness-optimization candidate (AC-876). A candidate is a proposed mechanism change to some target surface, carrying the hypothesis, the concrete changes, the fix and regression cases it is expected to move, its cost expectation, and its cross-language parity status. This schema is the single source of truth for both the Python and TypeScript autocontext packages.

schema_version.description: Schema version for forward compatibility. Always 1 for this revision.

candidate_id.description: Stable unique identifier for this candidate.

parent_frontier_id.description: Identifier of the frontier this candidate descends from. May be empty for a root candidate.

mechanism_name.description: Human-readable name of the mechanism this candidate changes.

target_surface.description: The surface of the harness this candidate targets.

hypothesis.description: The falsifiable claim about why this change should help.

changes.description: Description of the concrete changes this candidate makes.

changed_artifacts.description: Paths or identifiers of the artifacts this candidate modifies.

fix_cases.description: Named seeds, tasks, or traces this candidate is expected to improve.

regression_cases.description: Named seeds, tasks, or traces this candidate is expected to keep flat.

observed.description: Smoke, replay, or dry-run evidence gathered so far. May be empty at proposal time.

validation_plan.description: How this candidate will be validated before promotion.

extra_calls.description: Expected additional model or tool calls per run.

extra_seconds.description: Expected additional wall-clock seconds per run.

cost_expectation.description: Expected marginal cost of adopting this candidate.

leakage_scope.description: What data the proposer was allowed to inspect, for leakage auditing.

python.description: Implementation status of this candidate in the Python package.

typescript.description: Implementation status of this candidate in the TypeScript package.

schema_hash.description: Content hash of the shared schema the two implementations agree on.

parity.description: Cross-language parity status for this candidate.
